# Estimating Orange, RHD Cars ≥ 10 Years Old

## Step-by-step breakdown

### 1. Global car fleet
~**1.4 billion** passenger cars worldwide.

### 2. Right-hand-drive (RHD) fraction
Key RHD markets: Japan (~78M), UK (~33M), India (~45M), Australia (~20M), Thailand, Indonesia, Malaysia, South Africa, New Zealand, etc.
→ Roughly **270 million RHD cars** (~19% of global fleet)

### 3. Fraction ≥ 10 years old
Japan scraps cars relatively fast (shaken inspections); India's fleet is young and growing; UK/Australia are more typical.
→ Weighted average: ~**50%** → **~135 million** RHD cars ≥ 10 years old

### 4. Fraction that are orange
Orange is one of the rarest car colors. Industry color reports (Axalta, PPG) typically show:
- Current orange share: ~0.5–1%
- But for cars made **before ~2014**, orange was rarer (~0.3–0.5%), especially in RHD markets where white/silver dominate (hot climates of India, SE Asia)

→ I'll use **~0.35%** for this older cohort

### Final calculation

$$135{,}000{,}000 \times 0.0035 \approx 470{,}000$$

## **My estimate: 500,000**

# Estimating Orange, RHD Cars ≥ 10 Years Old

## Step-by-step breakdown

### 1. Total cars worldwide
~**1.4 billion**

### 2. Right-hand drive (RHD) fraction
Key RHD markets: Japan (~78M), India (~55M), UK (~33M), Australia (~20M), Indonesia (~20M), Thailand (~15M), Malaysia (~15M), South Africa (~12M), others (~25M).
**Total RHD ≈ 275M → ~20% of world fleet**

### 3. Fraction at least 10 years old (in RHD countries)
Japan scraps cars aggressively (~30–35% are 10+ yrs); India and Southeast Asia have many old vehicles but also rapid recent growth; UK/Australia ~45–50% are 10+ yrs.
**Weighted average ≈ 40–45% → ~110–120 million RHD cars ≥10 yrs old**

### 4. Fraction that are orange
Orange is consistently one of the rarest car colors. Modern reports (PPG/Axalta) put it at ~0.5–1% today, but **10+ years ago it was even rarer (~0.3%)**. In Asian and African RHD markets, color preferences skew even more conservatively.
**Estimated orange share of older fleet ≈ 0.3%**

### Final calculation

$$110{,}000{,}000 \times 0.003 = 330{,}000$$

## **My estimate: 350,000**

# Estimating Orange, RHD, 10+ Year-Old Cars Worldwide

## Step-by-step breakdown

### 1. Right-Hand-Drive (RHD) passenger car fleet
| Country/Region | Passenger cars (approx.) |
|---|---|
| Japan | 62 million |
| UK | 33 million |
| India | 40 million |
| Australia | 15 million |
| Indonesia, Thailand, Malaysia | ~31 million |
| South Africa, NZ, Pakistan, others | ~23 million |
| **Total RHD cars** | **~204 million** |

### 2. Fraction that are ≥10 years old
Averaging across these markets (Japan skews younger ~35%; India, Australia skew older ~50%), a weighted average is roughly **42%**, giving:
> **~86 million** RHD cars that are 10+ years old

### 3. Fraction that are orange
Orange currently represents ~1% of **new** car sales, but for cars built **before ~2015**, it was far rarer (~0.3–0.5%). Asian markets (Japan, India, SE Asia) skew even more heavily toward white/silver/black. A reasonable weighted average for this older RHD cohort is about **0.35%**.

# Estimating Orange, RHD, 10+ Year-Old Cars Worldwide

## Building Up the Estimate

### 1. Global car fleet
~**1.4 billion** passenger cars worldwide.

### 2. Right-Hand Drive (RHD) fraction
Key RHD markets: Japan (~78M), UK (~33M), India (~37M), Australia (~20M), Thailand/Indonesia/Malaysia (~47M), South Africa (~12M), plus smaller markets.
**Total RHD: ~260 million** (~19% of global fleet)

### 3. Fraction that are 10+ years old
This varies — Japan's *shaken* inspection pushes cars out faster (~35% are 10+), while developing RHD nations skew older (~60%). The UK/Australia sit around 45–50%.
**Weighted average: ~48% → ~125 million RHD cars aged 10+**

### 4. Fraction that are orange
Orange is one of the rarest production colors. PPG/Axalta data shows orange at ~0.5–1% for *recent* cars, but for cars built **before ~2015**, it was rarer (~0.2–0.4%). Asian markets (huge share of RHD) skew even more heavily toward white/silver/black, pushing orange down further.
**Weighted average for these older RHD cars: ~0.3%**

## Final Calculation

$$125{,}000{,}000 \times 0.003 = 375{,}000$$

## **My estimate: 350,000**

## Step-by-Step Breakdown

Major RHD markets: Japan (~62M), UK (~33M), India (~45M), Australia (~20M), Indonesia (~17M), Malaysia (~16M), Thailand (~12M), South Africa (~12M), others (~33M).

### 2. Fraction that are ≥10 years old (~40–45%)
- Japan's fleet is younger (~28% ≥10 yrs) due to strict inspections
- UK/Australia ~40–50%
- Developing RHD markets (India, Pakistan) ~35–50%
- **Weighted total: ~100 million RHD cars aged 10+**

### 3. Fraction that are orange (~0.4% for this cohort)
Orange is consistently one of the rarest colors. Modern reports (Axalta/PPG) put it around 0.5–1% of **new** sales, but:
- In the 2000–2010 era it was rarer (~0.2–0.3%)
- It rose to ~0.5–0.8% around 2010–2015
- Blended average for the ≥10-year-old cohort: **~0.4%**

### Calculation

$$250M \times 0.42 \times 0.004 \approx 400{,}000$$

---

## **My estimate: 400,000**

# Estimating Orange, RHD, 10+ Year-Old Cars Worldwide

## Step-by-step breakdown

### 1. Global car fleet
~**1.4 billion** registered passenger cars worldwide.

### 2. Right-hand-drive (RHD) fraction
Major RHD markets (Japan ~78M, UK ~33M, India ~45M, Australia ~20M, Thailand ~15M, Indonesia ~17M, Malaysia ~15M, South Africa ~12M, others ~45M) sum to roughly **280 million**, or about **20%** of the global fleet.

### 3. Fraction that are ≥10 years old
This varies: Japan scraps cars quickly (~30–35% are 10+ yrs), India/developing RHD countries keep them longer (~50–55%), UK/Australia ~40–45%. Weighted across RHD countries, roughly **43%** of the RHD fleet is 10+ years old.

→ 280M × 0.43 ≈ **120 million** RHD cars aged 10+

### 4. Fraction that are orange
Orange is consistently one of the rarest car colors. Industry data (PPG/Axalta) puts orange at **~0.3–0.5%** of sales, and it was even rarer in the 2005–2014 era relevant here. Asian markets (Japan, India, Thailand) skew heavily white/black/silver, pulling the RHD-weighted average down.

→ Estimated orange share for this cohort: **~0.35%**

### 5. Final calculation

$$120{,}000{,}000 \times 0.0035 \approx 420{,}000$$

---

## My point estimate: **450,000**

~**1.4 billion** passenger cars worldwide.

Adjusting for age distribution (Japan scraps cars faster; developing RHD countries skew older), I estimate about **110 million** RHD cars are 10+ years old.

Orange is consistently one of the rarest car colors. In the 2004–2014 manufacturing era, white/black/silver/gray dominated (~75%). Orange typically captured only **~0.4–0.5%** of sales — even lower in color-conservative markets like India. I use **0.45%**.

### Final Calculation

$$110{,}000{,}000 \times 0.0045 \approx 495{,}000$$

## **My estimate: 500,000**

## My Point Estimate: **300,000**

### Step 1: Total Cars Worldwide
~**1.4 billion** passenger cars globally.

### Step 2: Right-Hand-Drive (RHD) Fraction (~18%)
Key RHD markets: Japan (~62M), UK (~33M), India (~45M), Australia (~20M), Indonesia (~17M), Malaysia (~15M), Thailand (~12M), South Africa (~8M), others (~25M).
→ **~240 million** RHD cars

RHD countries skew slightly *newer* than the global average because India and Southeast Asia have rapidly growing fleets. I estimate ~45% of RHD cars are 10+ years old.

Orange is consistently one of the rarest car colors. Industry color reports (PPG, Axalta) typically place orange at **0.5% or below** for *new* cars, and it was even rarer pre-2015. White/silver dominate RHD markets like India (60–70%), further suppressing the orange share. For the surviving 10+ year-old fleet, I estimate **~0.3%**.

### Final Calculation
$$108{,}000{,}000 \times 0.003 \approx \boxed{300{,}000}$$

# Estimating Orange, RHD, 10+ Year-Old Cars Worldwide

## Step-by-step breakdown

### 1. Total cars worldwide
~**1.4 billion** registered passenger cars globally.

### 2. Right-Hand Drive (RHD) car population
Key RHD markets (Japan ~62M, India ~45M, UK ~33M, Australia ~15M, Thailand, Indonesia, Malaysia, South Africa, NZ, etc.) plus exported Japanese RHD vehicles in Africa/Asia.

**Total RHD cars: ~250 million** (~18% of global fleet)

### 3. Filtering for age ≥ 10 years
- Japan's strict *shaken* inspections skew younger (~40% are 10+)
- India/SE Asia/Africa skew older (~55–60% are 10+)

Orange is among the rarest car colors. Modern peak is ~0.5–1%, but for cars manufactured **before ~2015**, orange was much rarer (~0.2–0.5%). Using a weighted-average across the age distribution:

**~0.35–0.4% orange** for this cohort

$$105{,}000{,}000 \times 0.004 \approx 420{,}000$$

## **My estimate: 400,000**

# Estimating Orange, RHD Cars ≥ 10 Years Old

## Breaking Down the Factors

### 1. Global car fleet
~**1.4 billion** passenger cars worldwide.

### 2. Fraction ≥ 10 years old (~55%)
Average vehicle age is 12+ years in many large markets (US, Europe). Developing nations run cars longer, but newer markets like China/India skew younger. I estimate ~**770 million** cars are 10+ years old.

### 3. Fraction that are RHD (~19%)
Key RHD markets and their approximate fleets: Japan (~62M), UK (~33M), India (~50M), Australia (~20M), Thailand (~15M), Indonesia (~18M), Malaysia (~15M), South Africa (~12M), plus others (NZ, Pakistan, Kenya, Caribbean, etc.). Total ~**270M** RHD cars → ~19% of the global fleet.

Among older vehicles this drops slightly (Japan scraps/exports aggressively; India's fleet is young), so I use **~18%** for the 10+ year cohort → **~139 million** older RHD cars.

### 4. Fraction that are orange (~0.35%)

## Breaking Down the Factors

**Fraction that are RHD (~19%):** ~146 million
Key RHD markets and their older-car fleets: Japan (~35M), India (~18M), UK (~15M), Australia (~10M), Southeast Asia (~20M collectively), Africa/Pakistan/Others (~20M), plus ~3M Japanese imports in LHD countries.

**Fraction that are orange (~0.4–0.5%):** ~660,000
Orange is consistently one of the rarest production colors. In global color reports, it typically runs 0.5–1% for *new* cars in recent years, but was lower (0.2–0.5%) during the 2000s and earlier — the era most relevant to the 10+ year-old fleet. I use **~0.45%** as a weighted average for surviving older cars.

| Factor | Value |
|---|---|
| Cars worldwide | 1,400,000,000 |
| × ≥10 years old | × 0.55 |
| × RHD | × 0.19 |
| × Orange | × 0.0045 |
| **= Estimate** | **~660,000** |

## My Point Estimate

## **660,000**

### Calculation

$$220M \times 0.43 \times 0.0045 \approx 425{,}000$$

## My estimate

## **425,000**

## Step-by-Step Breakdown

### 1. Global car fleet

### 3. Fraction at least 10 years old
- Japan: ~37% (strict *shaken* inspections push cars out young)
- India: ~38% (market boomed recently, skewing newer)
- UK/Australia: ~50–55%
- Other RHD nations: ~55% (many run old used imports)

### 4. Fraction that are orange
Orange is consistently among the rarest colors (~0.3–0.5% globally for pre-2015 models). Crucially, in dominant RHD markets like **Japan and India**, white/silver/gray command 60–70%+ share, making orange even rarer (~0.1–0.2%). UK/Australia are closer to ~0.5%.

**Weighted average across RHD markets: ~0.3%**

### Final Calculation

$$117{,}000{,}000 \times 0.003 \approx 350{,}000$$

---

## **My estimate: 350,000**
